lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Hello there, can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where am I?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: This is the town of Lumbridge my friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you today?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Aye, not too bad thank you. Lovely weather in Gielinor this fine day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Weather?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Yes weather, you know.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: The state or condition of the atmosphere at a time and place, with respect to variables such as temperature, moisture, wind velocity, and barometric pressure.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Not just a pretty face eh? Ha ha ha.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know of any quests I can do?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: What kind of quest are you looking for?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Hmm.. dangerous you say? What sort of creatures are you looking to fight?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Big scary demons!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: You are a brave soul indeed.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Now that you mention it, I heard a rumour about a fortune-teller in Varrock who is rambling about some kind of greater evil.. sounds demon-like if you ask me.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Perhaps you could check it out if you are as brave as you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the tip, perhaps I will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've already killed the demon Delrith. He was merely a stain on my sword when I was finished with him!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Well done! However I'm sure if you search around the world you will find more challenging foes to slay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Vampyres!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Ha ha. I personally don't believe in such things. However, there is a man in Draynor Village who has been scaring the village folk with stories of vampyres.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: He's named Morgan and can be found in one of the village houses. Perhaps you could see what the matter is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the tip.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh I have already killed that nasty blood-sucking vampyre. Draynor will be safe now.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Yeah, yeah of course you did. Everyone knows vampyres are not real....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What! I did slay the beast..I really did.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: You're not fooling anyone you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ..Huh.. But... Hey! I did... believe what you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Small.. something small would be good.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Small? Small isn't really that dangerous though is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes it can be! There could be anything from an evil chicken to a poisonous spider. They attack in numbers you know!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Yes ok, point taken. Speaking of small monsters, I hear old Wizard Mizgog in the wizards' tower has just had all his beads taken by a gang of mischievous imps.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Sounds like it could be a quest for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I know of Mizgog and have already helped him with his imp problem. It took me ages to find those beads!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Imps will be imps!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe another time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something easy please, I'm new here.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: I can tell you about plenty of small easy tasks.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: The Lumbridge cook has been having problems, the Duke is confused over some strange talisman and on top of all that, poor lad Romeo in Varrock has girlfriend problems.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Lumbridge cook.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: It's funny really, the cook would forget his head if it wasn't screwed on. This time he forgot to get ingredients for the Duke's birthday cake.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Perhaps you could help him? You will probably find him in the Lumbridge Castle kitchen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I shall go speak with him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have already helped the cook in Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Oh yes, so you have. I am sure the Duke will be pleased.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Duke's strange talisman.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Well the Duke of Lumbridge has found a strange talisman that no one seems to understand. Perhaps you could help him? You can probably find him upstairs in Lumbridge Castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds mysterious. I may just do that. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have already solved the Rune mysteries.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Ah excellent. Thank you very much adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Romeo and his girlfriend please.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Romeo in Varrock needs help with finding his beloved Juliet, you may be able to help him out.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Unless of course you manage to find Juliet first in which case she has probably lost Romeo.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, ok. Romeo is in Varrock?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Yes you can't miss him, he's wandering aimlessly in the square.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, I've already helped Romeo in the best possible way I can...

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: ...How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He thinks Juliet is dead...

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Well.. ok.. well done... I think...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm a thinker rather than fighter, anything skill orientated?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Skills play a big part when you want to progress in knowledge throughout Gielinor. I know of a few skill- related quests that can get you started.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: You may be able to help out Fred the farmer who is in need of someones crafting expertise.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Or, there's always Doric the dwarf who needs an errand running for him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Fred the farmer please.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: You can find Fred next to the field of sheep in Lumbridge. Perhaps you should go and speak with him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, maybe I will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have already helped Fred the farmer. I sheared his sheep and made 20 balls of wool for him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He wouldn't let me kill his chickens though.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Lumbridge chickens do make good target practice.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: You will have to wait until he isn't looking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Doric the dwarf.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Doric the dwarf is located north of Falador. He might be able to help you with smithing. You should speak to him. He may let you use his anvils.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the tip.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've been to see Doric already. He was happy to let me use his anvils after I ran a small errand for him.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Oh good, Thank you [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to do all kinds of things, do you know of anything like that?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Of course I do. Gielinor is a huge place you know, now let me think...

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Hetty the witch in Rimmington might be able to offer help in the ways of magical abilities..

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Also, pirates are currently docked in Port Sarim, Where pirates are, treasure is never far away...

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Or you could go help out Ernest who got lost in Draynor Manor, spooky place that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Hetty the witch.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Hetty the witch can be found in Rimmington, south of Falador. She's currently working on some new potions. Perhaps you could give her a hand? She might be able to offer help with your magical abilities.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok thanks, let's hope she doesn't turn me into a potato or something..

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have already been to see Hetty, she gave me super cosmic powers after I helped out with her potion! I could probably destroy you with a single thought!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Did she really?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No not really...

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Right.....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Pirate's Treasure.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: RedBeard Frank in Port Sarim's bar, the Rusty Anchor, might be able to tell you about the rumored treasure that is buried somewhere in Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds adventurous, I may have to check that out. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yarr! I already found the booty!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Yarr indeed my friend. A most excellent find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yarr!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Yarrr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: YARRR!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Right, that's enough of that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ..Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Ernest please.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: The best place to start would be at the gate to Draynor Manor. There you will find Veronica who will be able to tell you more.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: I suggest you tread carefully in that place; it's haunted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like fun. I've never been to a Haunted Manor before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I found Ernest already. Professor Oddenstein had turned him into a chicken!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: A chicken!?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah a chicken. It could have been worse though.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Very true, poor guy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get a haircut like yours?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Yes, it does look like you need a hairdresser.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh thanks!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: No problem. The hairdresser in Falador will probably be able to sort you out.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: The Lumbridge general store sells useful maps if you don't know the way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's up?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: I assume the sky is up..

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You assume?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Yeah, unfortunately I don't seem to be able to look up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy your stick?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: It's not a stick! I'll have you know it's a very powerful staff!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Show me what it can do!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Um..It's a bit low on power at the moment..

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a stick isn't it?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: ...Ok it's a stick.. But only while I save up for a staff. Zaff in Varrock square sells them in his shop.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well good luck with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have anything of value which I can have?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Are you asking for free stuff?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... er... yes.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: No I do not have anything I can give you. If I did have anything of value I wouldn't want to give it away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your shoe lace is untied.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: No it's not!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No you're right. I have nothing to back that up.

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Fool! Leave me alone!

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: Hello there, can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what happened in the castle cellar?

lines.speaker: Gee
lines.text: I heard the wall collapsed or something. I don't know why. Perhaps you could get to the bottom of this mystery!
